title: Raw materialized views mv_lot_cost / mv_lot_cost_by_rule are SELECT-granted to authenticated and carry NO RLS — tenant A reads every tenant's COGS/financials directly
where: supabase/migrations/20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql H4, lines 645-646 (`grant select on mv_lot_cost to authenticated;` / `grant select on mv_lot_cost_by_rule to authenticated;`). The matviews are created at lines 616-631; a matview cannot carry RLS and materializes as owner, so the only gate is the table grant.
repro: Replay all migrations. Seed two tenants A,B each with a green lot (A=JC-100, B=JC-200) + cost graph, `refresh materialized view mv_lot_cost`. Then as tenant A (asTenant(h,A,...)) run `select green_lot_code, tenant_id, total_cost, cost_per_kg_green from mv_lot_cost`. Result returns BOTH rows including B's: [{green_lot_code:'JC-100',tenant_id:A},{green_lot_code:'JC-200',tenant_id:B}]. A reads B's green lot codes and per-kg COGS. This is the §6.1 '#1 financial leak'. The probe test passes only because its matview block (p4s0_tenant_isolation.db.test.ts:281-302) reads through the mv_lot_cost_secure wrapper whenever it exists, never the still-granted raw matview — so the leak survives the acceptance gate. The migration's own H4 comment (lines 642-644) admits the raw grant is a knowingly-left 'residual'. Verified empirically via an ad-hoc PGlite replay (now removed): RAW mv_lot_cost as A returned B's JC-200 row; mv_lot_cost_secure as A correctly returned only JC-100.
fix: Revoke the raw-matview SELECT grant from authenticated and expose ONLY the security_barrier wrappers: delete lines 645-646 (`grant select on mv_lot_cost/mv_lot_cost_by_rule to authenticated`), keeping the `_secure` grants (647-648). The raw matviews stay owner-only; cogs_* ports already read them as SECURITY DEFINER/owner-bypass or via current_tenant_id() filters and are unaffected. Because s7_costing.db.test.ts:308-313 and :435-438 assert `has_table_privilege('authenticated','mv_lot_cost','select')` / `mv_lot_cost_by_rule`, those two phase-1 assertions must be updated in the same change to target mv_lot_cost_secure / mv_lot_cost_by_rule_secure (the new authenticated read path) — or the suite reds. Owner author (Migrate agent) owns both the migration grant edit and the s7 test reconciliation.

title: advance_processing_stage() lets tenant A rewrite (and destroy) tenant B's lot — no tenant clamp on the SECURITY DEFINER UPDATE
where: supabase/migrations/20260622094000_drying_reposo.sql:436-525 (the live definition of advance_processing_stage; earlier copies at 20260621120000_pipeline_fixes.sql:137 and 20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:421). Granted to authenticated. Migration 3 (20260701092000) §I explicitly leaves it untouched, claiming existing RPCs 'already stamp tenant_id correctly via the column default' — false for a parent-row UPDATE-by-code.
repro: Seed tenants A,B. Insert lots (B,'JC-200','cherry',999kg) and (A,'JC-200','cherry',10kg) — per-tenant lot codes make BOTH tenants holding 'JC-200' the COMMON case (both counters start near JC-001). As A: select advance_processing_stage('JC-200','green',5,now(),'d',1,'k1'). It does NOT throw. The body does `select stage,current_kg from lots where code='JC-200'` and `update lots set stage=..., current_kg=... where code='JC-200'` with NO `and tenant_id=current_tenant_id()`; SECURITY DEFINER runs as owner and bypasses RLS. RESULT (observed): the UPDATE matched BOTH rows — B's lot went {cherry,999kg} -> {green,5kg}, wiping 994kg of B's inventory and corrupting its stage/COGS. With only B owning JC-200, B's row is mutated cherry->green,77kg while A owns no such lot. The lot_event append is tenant-A-stamped, so B never even sees the audit trail of the mutation.
fix: In advance_processing_stage add `v_tenant uuid := current_tenant_id(); if v_tenant is null then raise … insufficient_privilege; end if;` at the top, and tenant-qualify EVERY lots access: the existence `select … from lots where code=p_lot_code and tenant_id=v_tenant`, the `update lots … where code=p_lot_code and tenant_id=v_tenant`, and the idempotency `select … from lot_event where idempotency_key=… and tenant_id=v_tenant`. Re-assert AD-8 grants. Add a probe write-isolation case calling the FULL 7-arg signature (not an arity-mismatch overload) as A against B's lot and asserting B's lot row is byte-identical after.

title: approve_pay_line(bigint) lets tenant A approve tenant B's payroll line AND advance B's pay period — cross-tenant financial write
where: supabase/migrations/20260622108000_payroll.sql:782-819 (approve_pay_line(bigint), granted to authenticated at ~line 970-area). Migration 3 §I3 adds a tenant-clamped approve_pay_line(text) overload for the probe, but the underlying bigint RPC is left unguarded and still granted — a hostile caller invokes the bigint form directly, bypassing the overload entirely.
repro: Seed tenants A,B. Give B a worker, a pay_period 'pp-B' (status 'calculated'), and a pay_line (status 'calculated'); capture its id (bigint identity = GLOBAL sequence, so B's ids are reachable by guessing/enumeration, no collision with A). As A: select approve_pay_line(<B_line_id>::bigint). It does NOT throw. Body: `select status from pay_line where id=p_pay_line_id` then `update pay_line set status='approved' where id=p_pay_line_id` and `update pay_period set status='approved' where pp.id=(select pay_period_id from pay_line where id=p_pay_line_id)` — all with NO tenant filter. OBSERVED: B's pay_line went calculated->approved AND B's pay_period calculated->approved. A approved B's payroll run, unlocking B's disbursement close-out.
fix: Tenant-clamp the bigint RPC itself (this is the real write door): `v_tenant := current_tenant_id(); … select status from pay_line where id=p_pay_line_id and tenant_id=v_tenant; … update pay_line … where id=p_pay_line_id and tenant_id=v_tenant; update pay_period pp set … where pp.id=(select pay_period_id from pay_line where id=p_pay_line_id and tenant_id=v_tenant) and pp.tenant_id=v_tenant …`. Same fix for reverse_pay_line(20260622108000:954) and record_disbursement/reverse-disbursement (`update disbursement … where id=p_disbursement_id` at :1046). Add a probe case calling approve_pay_line(<bigint>) directly as A against B's line.

title: release_qc_hold() lets tenant A clear tenant B's open QC hold — re-opening B's quarantined lot for commerce
where: supabase/migrations/20260622096000_qc_cupping.sql:497-522 (release_qc_hold, granted to authenticated). Not exercised by the probe (the probe's place_qc_hold case relies on a 2-arg arity mismatch; release_qc_hold is a separate, fully-callable RPC). The qc_holds block trigger does NOT stop the DEFINER UPDATE (confirmed).
repro: Seed tenants A,B. B owns green lot 'JC-200' (lots+green_lots) and an OPEN qc_hold (released_at null) on it. As A: select release_qc_hold('JC-200',now(),'devA',9,'rk-1'). Does NOT throw. Body: `update qc_holds set released_at=p_occurred_at, released_by=p_device_id where green_lot_code=p_green_lot_code and released_at is null` with NO tenant filter (and per-tenant green-lot codes mean 'JC-200' collides across tenants). OBSERVED: B's hold released_at flipped null -> timestamp. A cleared B's quality quarantine, allowing B's defective lot to be reserved/shipped.
fix: Add `v_tenant := current_tenant_id()` and `and tenant_id=v_tenant` to the qc_holds UPDATE predicate (and fail closed when v_tenant is null). Add a probe write-isolation case for release_qc_hold as A against B's open hold, asserting B's hold is still open afterward.

title: Systemic: ~20 caller-facing SECURITY DEFINER write-RPCs mutate scoped rows by bare code/id with no tenant clamp — migration 3 §I's 'they already stamp correctly' assumption is false for any UPDATE-by-key path
where: Pattern across the RPC surface; grep `update <scoped_table> … where <key>=p_arg` with no tenant_id: advance_processing_stage, approve_pay_line, reverse_pay_line, record_disbursement-reversal (20260622108000), release_qc_hold (20260622096000), record_dispatch_ack / dispatch UPDATEs (20260622104000:484,583,587), record_attendance->_resync_worker_attendance and set/resync_worker_crew (20260622090000:487,510), update crews set season (20260622090000:882), record_weigh_in's `update lots/workers` (20260622102000:320,391). The column-default tenant-stamp only covers INSERTs; composite FKs only protect INSERTs into child tables. UPDATEs to the parent (lots/workers/crews/pay_line/qc_holds/dispatch_run) and the existence SELECTs in front of them have NO tenant predicate and run as RLS-bypassing owner.
repro: Generic: as tenant A, call any of the above RPCs with a key (lot code / worker id / crew id / pay_line id / dispatch_run id) belonging to tenant B. The pre-flight `select … where id=p_arg` resolves B's row (owner bypasses RLS) and the subsequent UPDATE mutates it. Confirmed concretely for advance_processing_stage, approve_pay_line, release_qc_hold above; the remainder share the identical shape. NB: paths that then INSERT into a composite-FK child (e.g. start_ferment_batch, materialize_green_lot) are accidentally rejected by the composite FK / tenant-clamped mass trigger — but the UPDATE-only paths have no such backstop.
fix: Do NOT rely on the column default for write-RPCs. Each caller-facing definer RPC must resolve `v_tenant := current_tenant_id()`, fail closed on null, and tenant-qualify every existence SELECT, UPDATE, and idempotency lookup with `and tenant_id=v_tenant`. Then extend the §8 probe's write-isolation matrix to call the FULL signatures of these RPCs (not arity-mismatch shims) as A against B-owned keys, asserting (a) rejection and (b) B's subject row byte-identical after — the current probe only covers 5 RPCs and several of those pass only via an incidental arity/FK accident, not an actual tenant check.

title: Raw mv_lot_cost / mv_lot_cost_by_rule SELECT grant retained — authenticated tenant A reads every tenant's cost-per-kg, bypassing the _secure wrapper (the #1 financial leak the slice was meant to close, and the probe does NOT catch it)
where: supabase/migrations/20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql §H4 lines 641-648 (`grant select on mv_lot_cost to authenticated;` and `... mv_lot_cost_by_rule ...` RETAINED). Probe blind spot: src/test/db/p4s0_tenant_isolation.db.test.ts lines 289-292 read `<mv>_secure` whenever it exists, so the raw-grant path is never exercised → false green.
repro: Empirically reproduced (PGlite, full migration replay). Seed tenants A,B; give B a green lot JC-001 + a cost_entry(driver='task',allocation_rule='direct-labor',target_kind='lot',target_code='JC-001',amount_usd=500); `refresh materialized view mv_lot_cost`. Then as tenant A (`asTenant(h,A,...)`) run `select tenant_id from mv_lot_cost` → returns B's row (observed `['bbbb…bbbb']`). `has_table_privilege('authenticated','mv_lot_cost','select')` returns TRUE. The `mv_lot_cost_secure` wrapper correctly returns [] for A, but nothing forces reads through it — a matview carries no RLS, so the surviving raw grant is a full cross-tenant COGS read for any authenticated caller.
fix: Follow plan §6.1 step 2 verbatim: `revoke select on mv_lot_cost from authenticated;` and `revoke select on mv_lot_cost_by_rule from authenticated;` (keep grants ONLY on mv_lot_cost_secure / mv_lot_cost_by_rule_secure). The cogs_* ports already read the raw matview as DEFINER-free invoker fns but self-filter by current_tenant_id(), so they keep working without the table grant only if they remain the read path — verify they don't need the grant (security invoker fn reading an owner-owned matview with no RLS does not require the caller to hold the grant when the fn is owned by a privileged role; if it does, make them security definer or read the _secure view). Update the pre-existing grant-posture assertion src/test/db/s7_costing.db.test.ts:308-316 (and :84 raw read) to assert authenticated can select mv_lot_cost_secure and CANNOT select raw mv_lot_cost — that test is a phase-1 posture check, not an external contract, and is the only thing the migration cited to justify retaining the leak.

title: weigh_event hash-chain interleaves across tenants — B's genesis weigh event chains its prev_hash off A's head when they share stream_key 'weigh:<lot_code>' (per-tenant lot codes guarantee the collision); the §6.4 BEFORE-INSERT tenant assert is also missing on this ledger
where: supabase/migrations/20260622102000_weigh_capture.sql lines 135-154 (`weigh_event_set_hash`): head-select is `where e.stream_key = new.stream_key order by e.device_seq desc` with NO `and e.tenant_id = new.tenant_id`, and no tenant-mismatch assert. The P4-S0 enforce migration (20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql §C) rewrote lot_event/worker_stream_event/attendance_event set_hash with the tenant-scoped head-select + assert but OMITTED weigh_event — even though weigh_event is in DIRECT_TENANT_TABLES (tenantTables.ts:36) and §6.4 names all FOUR ledgers.
repro: Empirically reproduced (PGlite). Both tenants own lot JC-001 (LOCKED per-tenant codes). A inserts weigh_event(stream_key='weigh:JC-001', device_seq=1) → prev_hash NULL (genesis). B then inserts its FIRST weigh_event(stream_key='weigh:JC-001', device_seq=2) → observed prev_hash = non-null (49df8687…), i.e. B's genesis chained off A's hash. Real path: record_weigh_in() builds stream_key as `'weigh:' || v_lot` (line 374), so any two tenants weighing their own JC-001 collide. Result: interleaved cross-tenant chain = the §6.4 'data-integrity corruption' (verify_chain breaks for both; B's prev_hash silently encodes A's event existence).
fix: Rewrite weigh_event_set_hash to mirror the other three ledgers in 20260701092000 §C: add the BEFORE-INSERT assert `if new.tenant_id is distinct from current_tenant_id() then raise exception ... using errcode='insufficient_privilege'; end if;` and tenant-scope the head-select `... where e.stream_key = new.stream_key and e.tenant_id = new.tenant_id order by e.device_seq desc limit 1`. Add the composite tenant-scoped uniqueness index `create unique index if not exists weigh_event_tenant_idem_ux on weigh_event (tenant_id, idempotency_key) where idempotency_key is not null;` for parity with §C1. Re-assert grants (revoke execute ... from public) per AD-8. Add a probe assertion: B's genesis weigh event for a shared 'weigh:<code>' stream_key has prev_hash IS NULL.

title: verify_chain(stream_key) is not tenant-clamped — DEFINER + granted to authenticated, it walks every tenant's lot_event rows for a shared stream_key (e.g. the literal 'activity' key, or a per-tenant-collidable lot code), leaking cross-tenant event existence/ordering and returning a spurious FALSE that poisons each tenant's own integrity check
where: supabase/migrations/20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql lines 297-330 (`verify_chain`): loop is `select * from lot_event e where e.stream_key = verify_chain.stream_key order by e.device_seq` with NO tenant filter; DEFINER, granted to authenticated (line 557). The P4-S0 band never redefines it. Plan §6.3 + the §6.4 table row 4 explicitly mark this '[review-hardened] scope loop and tenant_id = current_tenant_id()' as REQUIRED — it was not done.
repro: Empirically reproduced (PGlite). Insert a lot_event for stream_key 'shared-stream' under tenant A (device_seq 1) and another under tenant B (device_seq 100), each via its own claim so the now-clamped _set_hash builds two independent NULL-prev chains. As tenant A, `select verify_chain('shared-stream')` → returns FALSE (observed), because the loop interleaves both tenants' rows ordered by global device_seq and sees a second NULL-prev row mid-walk. This both (a) lets A confirm B has events on a shared stream_key (real keys: the literal 'activity' at lines 473/499 shared by all tenants, and bare per-tenant lot codes used as stream_key by record_cherry_intake) and (b) makes A's own valid chain report tampered.
fix: Add `and e.tenant_id = current_tenant_id()` to the verify_chain loop's where-clause (keep it SECURITY DEFINER per MED-3 so it still sees the whole of the caller's own stream; do NOT flip to security_invoker — a partial-visibility walk can return a spurious TRUE). Re-assert AD-8 grants on the `create or replace`. Ship a regression test: two tenants with the same stream_key each get verify_chain()=TRUE for their own chain, and A's verify_chain over a B-only stream_key returns TRUE/empty rather than reflecting B's rows. Note: the sibling DEFINER aggregators compute_pay_period (payroll.sql:534, unfiltered `for r in select … from workers` — runs payroll over ALL tenants) and generate_dispatch (crew_dispatch.sql:375) are out of the strict matview-ledger lens but are the same un-clamped-DEFINER class flagged in §6.3 and write financial pay_line rows; flag for the inventory/rls lens.

title: advance_processing_stage writes BOTH tenants' lots via bare `where code = p_lot_code` (cross-tenant WRITE)
where: supabase/migrations/20260622094000_drying_reposo.sql:436-525 (latest def; granted to authenticated at 20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:560). M3 (20260701092000) does NOT redefine or tenant-clamp it.
repro: Seed tenants A and B; both own lot JC-001 (per-tenant codes GUARANTEE this collision). As A call `advance_processing_stage('JC-001','fermentation',90,now(),'devA',1,'adv-A-1')`. The fn is SECURITY DEFINER (runs as table owner, RLS bypassed) and its core mutation is `update lots set stage=p_to_stage, current_kg=... where code = p_lot_code` with NO tenant clause (line 514-517) — it matches and rewrites BOTH A's and B's JC-001 rows. Empirically verified: after the call, B's JC-001 row flipped stage cherry->fermentation and current_kg 100->90, with no error. A silently corrupts B's processing stage + mass (and thus EUDR mass-conservation). The pre-mutation reads (line 464 `select stage,current_kg from lots where code=p_lot_code`), the idempotency lookup (line 455 `lot_event where idempotency_key=...`, NOT tenant-qualified unlike M3's record_cherry_intake), the reposo-gate evidence reads (502-505), and record_lot_event(p_lot_code,...) (519) are all bare-code/bare-key too.
fix: In M3, redefine advance_processing_stage to resolve v_tenant := current_tenant_id() (raise if null), tenant-qualify the idempotency_key (v_tenant::text||':'||p_idempotency_key) like record_cherry_intake does, and add `and tenant_id = v_tenant` to EVERY lots/lot_event/drying_assignments/moisture_readings/processing_batches read AND to the `update lots ... where code = p_lot_code` (-> `and tenant_id = v_tenant`). This is the same blind spot as the others below: the composite FK constrains writes through the FK but does nothing for a definer's own bare-code UPDATE.

title: reposo_status(p_lot_code) aggregates every tenant's moisture readings + stage events for a shared code (cross-tenant READ + corrupts the milling gate)
where: supabase/migrations/20260622094000_drying_reposo.sql:183-279 (SECURITY DEFINER, granted to authenticated). Reached directly, via v_reposo_status view (283-290), via the lots_enforce_reposo_gate BEFORE-UPDATE trigger (533-560 -> reposo_status(new.code)), and via advance_processing_stage (506). NOT rewritten by M3.
repro: Seed A and B; both own lot JC-001. Give B ten in-band moisture_readings on JC-001; give A zero. As A call `select reading_count from reposo_status('JC-001')`. Because reposo_status is SECURITY DEFINER (RLS bypassed) and every internal query keys on bare `where lot_code = p_lot_code` / `where stream_key = p_lot_code` with no tenant filter (lines 218-222, 235-237, 250-252), A receives reading_count=10 — all of them B's. Empirically verified (expected 0, got 10). This leaks B's drying data to A AND drives A's reposo/milling gate (a safety invariant) off B's readings, so A could be blocked-or-cleared to mill based on a different farm's moisture.
fix: reposo_status must take/resolve a tenant. Simplest: add `v_tenant uuid := current_tenant_id()` inside and append `and tenant_id = v_tenant` to all four queries (moisture_readings x2, lot_event stage-advance head-select, and the count). The lots_enforce_reposo_gate trigger and advance_processing_stage then call the clamped version. The v_reposo_status view already runs security_invoker so it self-filters once the function is clamped under the invoking tenant — but the function being SECURITY DEFINER means it MUST clamp internally regardless of caller.

title: verify_chain(stream_key) walks every tenant's events on a shared lot stream_key (cross-tenant READ + false integrity failure)
where: supabase/migrations/20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:297-330 (SECURITY DEFINER, granted authenticated at :557). NOT rewritten by M3, even though M3's lot_event_set_hash head-select was correctly tenant-scoped to (stream_key, tenant_id).
repro: Seed A and B; both own lot JC-001; each writes its OWN valid 3-event hash chain on lot_event with stream_key='JC-001' (record_cherry_intake/record_lot_event both use the bare lot code as stream_key, so the two tenants' chains share the same stream_key). As A call `select verify_chain('JC-001')`. verify_chain is SECURITY DEFINER and does `select * from lot_event where stream_key = verify_chain.stream_key order by device_seq` with NO tenant filter (line 309-312), so it interleaves A's and B's 6 events into one walk; the prev_hash linkage breaks across the boundary and it returns FALSE. Empirically verified (expected true, got false). A learns B's stream has events (existence/structure leak) and A's own valid ledger is reported tampered — undermining the audit guarantee the hash chain exists to provide.
fix: Add a tenant clause to verify_chain's loop: `where e.stream_key = verify_chain.stream_key and e.tenant_id = current_tenant_id()` (matching M3's lot_event_set_hash head-select, which already keys on (stream_key, tenant_id)). The people_system verify_chain (worker:/attendance: stream keys) is lower risk because those keys embed a tenant-scoped worker id rather than a per-tenant lot code, but for symmetry it should get the same clamp.

title: weigh_event ledger braids across tenants — B's hash chain links off A's head (shared stream_key under per-tenant lot codes)
where: supabase/migrations/20260622102000_weigh_capture.sql:135-154 (weigh_event_set_hash); NOT rebound by 20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql section C2 (which rebinds only lot_event/worker_stream_event/attendance_event)
repro: Seed tenants A,B. Both own lot 'JC-001' (per-tenant lot codes is the LOCKED decision → both legitimately mint JC-001). Insert weigh_event tenant_id=A stream_key='weigh:JC-001' device_seq=1, then tenant_id=B stream_key='weigh:JC-001' device_seq=2. weigh_event_set_hash() does `select e.hash from weigh_event e where e.stream_key=new.stream_key order by device_seq desc limit 1` with NO `and e.tenant_id=new.tenant_id`. Verified in-process: B's genesis row gets prev_hash=025390386dd6... (A's hash) instead of NULL. B's append-only ledger is cryptographically chained to A's events: B cannot be verified without reading A's rows, A's write cadence leaks into B's chain, and there is no BEFORE-INSERT `if new.tenant_id is distinct from current_tenant_id()` assert (the HIGH-3 guard C2 added to the other 3 ledgers is also missing here), so a forged tenant_id appends silently. Corroborated by sibling probe zz_vcprobe.db.test.ts WEIGH_EVENT (fails).
fix: In 20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql section C2, add a `create or replace function weigh_event_set_hash()` identical to the other three rebinds: (a) add `and e.tenant_id = new.tenant_id` to the head-select, and (b) prepend `if new.tenant_id is distinct from current_tenant_id() then raise exception ... using errcode='insufficient_privilege'; end if;` Re-assert AD-8 grants. weigh_event is in DIRECT_TENANT_TABLES (tenantTables.ts:36) so it is in scope — the omission is the bug.

title: verify_chain(stream_key) walks both tenants' interleaved events (SECURITY DEFINER, no tenant filter)
where: supabase/migrations/20260621092000_event_log_units_lot_graph.sql:297-327 and the attendance/worker variant 20260622090000_people_system.sql:402+; neither rebound by 20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql
repro: verify_chain is SECURITY DEFINER (bypasses RLS) and does `select * from lot_event e where e.stream_key=verify_chain.stream_key order by device_seq`, NO tenant qualifier. device_seq is globally unique (one shared lot_code_seq, per C1 comment). With per-tenant lot codes both A and B own JC-001 → both write stream_key='JC-001'; the walk interleaves A's and B's events ordered by global device_seq, so the prev_hash chain check breaks and verify_chain('JC-001') returns false for chains that are each individually valid — AND it reads B's event payloads while invoked by A. Corroborated by sibling probe zz_scratch_verifychain_leak.db.test.ts (fails).
fix: verify_chain must resolve the caller's tenant and filter the walk: add `and e.tenant_id = current_tenant_id()` to the select (and switch to SECURITY INVOKER, or keep DEFINER but clamp on current_tenant_id()). Apply to BOTH verify_chain definitions (lot_graph + people_system). Re-assert grants.

title: reposo_status(p_lot_code) counts the other tenant's moisture/drying rows (SECURITY DEFINER, no tenant filter)
repro: reposo_status is SECURITY DEFINER and resolves moisture_readings / drying_assignments by bare p_lot_code with no current_tenant_id() filter. Under per-tenant lot codes A and B both own JC-001; calling reposo_status('JC-001') as A aggregates B's moisture readings into A's reading_count / latest_moisture / readiness verdict — a cross-tenant read of operational data and a wrong dry-down decision. Corroborated by sibling probe zz_scratch_reposo_leak.db.test.ts (fails).
fix: Add `and tenant_id = current_tenant_id()` to every moisture_readings / drying_assignments / processing_batches lookup inside reposo_status (mirror the cogs_* ports in section H6 which do clamp). Sweep all other SECURITY DEFINER read/derive functions that resolve by bare lot_code or stream_key for the same omission.

title: Raw mv_lot_cost / mv_lot_cost_by_rule keep authenticated SELECT grant — any tenant reads every tenant's COGS via the raw matview
where: supabase/migrations/20260701092000_tenant_enforce_rls.sql:645-646 (grant select on mv_lot_cost / mv_lot_cost_by_rule to authenticated, H4)
repro: Matviews carry no RLS. The migration deliberately RETAINS the raw SELECT grant (H4 comment) so the AD-8 posture test stays green, exposing only the *_secure barrier views as the 'recommended' path. Verified: as tenant A, `select green_lot_code from mv_lot_cost` returns ['JC-100','JC-200'] (A's AND B's financials incl. total_cost); `select from mv_lot_cost_secure` correctly returns ['JC-100'] only. The probe's matview test reads <mv>_secure (because it exists) so the acceptance gate passes, masking the live raw read surface. Corroborated by sibling probe zz_vcprobe.db.test.ts (raw-grant + raw-read tests fail). NOTE: this is a documented design tradeoff, not an accident — flagging because it is a real surviving cross-tenant financial read for any direct `from mv_lot_cost`.
fix: Revoke SELECT on the raw matviews from authenticated (`revoke select on mv_lot_cost, mv_lot_cost_by_rule from authenticated`) and route all reads through *_secure / cogs_* ports; if the phase-1 AD-8 posture test asserts the raw grant, update that assertion to target the secure view instead. If Andres wants to keep the raw grant for posture-test reasons, accept the residual explicitly — but the app must never `from mv_lot_cost` directly.
